name: Wolfram Mathematica
description: A comprehensive computational platform for symbolic mathematics, numerical computation, visualisation, and algorithm development. The gold standard for analytical physics calculations.
whyUseIt: Mathematica can solve integrals, differential equations, and algebraic systems symbolically — giving you exact answers, not just numbers. It is widely used in theoretical physics for deriving results that would take pages of hand calculation.
fieldPosition: Theory and computation — used across all branches of physics for symbolic and numerical work
physicistWorkflow: Derive equations symbolically, solve them, plot results, and export to LaTeX for publication.
supervisorExpects: Fluency in symbolic computation and the ability to verify hand calculations computationally.
computationalImpact: Eliminates tedious algebra and catches errors in analytical derivations. Enables exploration of parameter spaces.
demoDescription: Solve the Schrödinger equation for a harmonic oscillator symbolically, plot the first five wave functions, and animate the time evolution of a superposition state.

vocabulary.definition: The computational engine that evaluates Mathematica expressions
An interactive document combining code, text, and output

beginnerMistakes: Using numerical approximations when exact symbolic solutions exist
Not simplifying expressions before evaluating them
